topics.title: print()
topics.summary: Displays a value in the Python output console.
topics.description:
Put **text**, numbers or variables inside the brackets. Strings must be surrounded by quotation marks.

For example, `python:print("Hello!")` displays a greeting.
topics.syntax: Use `python:print("Hello, world!")` to display a message.

topics.title: Variables
topics.summary: Named storage for a value that a program can use and change.
topics.description: A variable can hold text, numbers or other values. Give it a useful name so code is easy to understand.

topics.title: Strings
topics.summary: Text data written between quotation marks.
topics.description: Strings can contain letters, spaces and symbols. Python can display them, join them and store them in variables.

topics.title: For loops
topics.summary: Repeats indented code once for each item in a sequence.
topics.description: Use range() when you want to repeat code a particular number of times.

topics.title: range()
topics.summary: Creates a sequence of numbers, often used by a for loop.
topics.description: range(5) provides 0, 1, 2, 3 and 4. It creates five loop turns even though the final number is 4.

topics.title: HTML elements
topics.summary: The building blocks that describe content on a web page.
topics.description: Elements usually have an opening tag, content and a closing tag. Headings and paragraphs are common examples.

topics.title: Headings
topics.summary: Titles and subheadings represented by h1 through h6 tags.
topics.description: An h1 is normally the most important heading on a page. Smaller numbered headings introduce subsections.

topics.title: CSS properties
topics.summary: Settings that control how selected HTML content looks.
topics.description: A property is followed by a colon, a value and a semicolon inside a CSS rule.

topics.title: CSS selectors
topics.summary: Patterns that choose which HTML elements a CSS rule styles.
topics.description: Select a tag by name, a class with a dot or an id with a hash symbol.

topics.title: background-image
topics.summary: Displays an image behind an element's content.
topics.description: Provide an image address using url(). Other background properties control its size, position and repetition.

topics.title: Event blocks
topics.summary: Hat blocks that start scripts when something happens.
topics.description: A green-flag or key-pressed event starts the blocks connected beneath it.

topics.title: Motion blocks
topics.summary: Blue blocks that move or turn a sprite on the stage.
topics.description: Sprites can move steps, turn, glide, or change their x and y positions.

topics.title: Repeat blocks
topics.summary: Control blocks that run the blocks inside them several times.
topics.description: Set the repeat number to decide how many times the nested instructions run.
